ft_scalar.description: Outputs a DOE extraction line to create a Sentaurus Workbench extracted variable with the specified name and value. This command only produces output when the current Inspect script belongs to a Sentaurus Workbench project.

parameters.name: <variableName>
parameters.desc: Name of the Sentaurus Workbench variable to extract.

parameters.name: <variableValue>
parameters.desc: Value of the Sentaurus Workbench variable.

cv_write.description: Writes (exports) the data of the specified curves to a file in the given format. Supported output formats are plt, xgraph, and xmgr.

parameters.name: <type>
parameters.desc: Output format: "plt", "xgraph", or "xmgr".

parameters.name: <fileName>
parameters.desc: Name of the file to write.

parameters.name: <curveList>
parameters.desc: List of curve names to export.

fi_writeBitmap.description: Writes the current plot area to a PNG bitmap file.

parameters.name: <fileName>
parameters.desc: Name of the PNG file to write.

fi_writeEps.description: Writes the current plot area to an EPS (Encapsulated PostScript) file. If height or width is not specified, the actual plot size is used.

parameters.name: <fileName>
parameters.desc: Name of the EPS file to write.

parameters.name: ?orientation?
parameters.desc: Optional. Image orientation: "portrait" (default) or "landscape".

parameters.name: ?height?
parameters.desc: Optional. Height of the saved image in pixels.

parameters.name: ?width?
parameters.desc: Optional. Width of the saved image in pixels.

fi_writePs.description: Writes the current plot area to a PostScript file with configurable page size, image dimensions, and page offset. If height or width is not specified, the actual plot size is used.

parameters.name: <fileName>
parameters.desc: Name of the PostScript file to write.

parameters.name: ?orientation?
parameters.desc: Optional. Image orientation: "portrait" (default) or "landscape".

parameters.name: ?printSize?
parameters.desc: Optional. Page size: "US LETTER" (default), "DIN_A3", or "DIN_A4".

parameters.name: ?height?
parameters.desc: Optional. Height of the saved image in the unit specified by sizeUnit.

parameters.name: ?width?
parameters.desc: Optional. Width of the saved image in the unit specified by sizeUnit.

parameters.name: ?offsetHeight?
parameters.desc: Optional. Vertical page offset in sizeUnit.

parameters.name: ?offsetWidth?
parameters.desc: Optional. Horizontal page offset in sizeUnit.

parameters.name: ?sizeUnit?
parameters.desc: Optional. Unit for height, width, and offset values: "i" (inch, default), "m" (millimeter), or "c" (centimeter).

graph_load.description: Loads a previously saved Inspect save file. All currently loaded projects are deleted before the new file is loaded.

parameters.name: <fileName>
parameters.desc: Name of the save file to load.

graph_write.description: Saves the current Inspect state (including all loaded projects, curves, and settings) to a save file.

parameters.name: <fileName>
parameters.desc: Name of the file to save.

param_load.description: Loads a parameter file into Inspect. Parameter files store extracted values and settings for reuse across sessions.

parameters.name: <fileName>
parameters.desc: Name of the parameter file to load.

param_write.description: Saves current parameters to a parameter file for later reuse.

parameters.name: <fileName>
parameters.desc: Name of the parameter file to write.

proj_getDataSet.description: Returns a list of all values from the specified dataset in a project. If no dataset is found, an empty list is returned.

parameters.name: <projectName>
parameters.desc: Name of the project containing the dataset.

parameters.name: <dataSetId>
parameters.desc: Dataset name, including its group name if applicable (e.g., "node_A data_1").

proj_getList.description: Returns a list of all currently loaded project names. If no projects are loaded, an empty list is returned.

proj_getNodeList.description: Returns a list of group (node) names in the specified project. If no groups are found, an empty list is returned.

parameters.name: <projectName>
parameters.desc: Name of the project.

proj_load.description: Loads a data file and creates a new project. The base name of the file is used as the project name.

parameters.name: <fileName>
parameters.desc: Name of the data file to load.

proj_unload.description: Deletes a project and all curves associated with it from the current Inspect session.

parameters.name: <projectName>
parameters.desc: Name of the project to unload.

proj_write.description: Writes a project and its datasets to a specified file.

parameters.name: <projectName>
parameters.desc: Name of the project to write.

parameters.name: <fileName>
parameters.desc: Name of the output file.

cv_create.description: Creates a curve with the given name using the specified x and y datasets without displaying it. The datasets must already be loaded. The data path consists of the project name, optional group name, and dataset name.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Unique name for the new curve.

parameters.name: <xDataPath>
parameters.desc: X-dataset data path (e.g., "nmos_des time").

parameters.name: <yDataPath>
parameters.desc: Y-dataset data path (e.g., "nmos_des Gate OuterVoltage").

parameters.name: ?axis?
parameters.desc: Optional. Axis to use: "y" (default) or "y2".

cv_createDS.description: Creates a curve with the given name using the specified datasets and immediately displays it. This is the same as cv_create except the curve is shown on the plot.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Unique name for the new curve.

parameters.name: <xDataPath>
parameters.desc: X-dataset data path.

parameters.name: <yDataPath>
parameters.desc: Y-dataset data path.

parameters.name: ?axis?
parameters.desc: Optional. Axis to use: "y" (default) or "y2".

cv_createDS.example: cv_createDS Id "nmos_des Gate OuterVoltage" "nmos_des Drain TotalCurrent"

cv_createFromScript.description: Creates a curve using the given name and explicit data values. If the number of x and y values differ, the curve uses the smaller count. Curves created with this command are stored in AuxProject.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Unique name for the new curve.

parameters.name: <xdata>
parameters.desc: Space-separated list of x-axis data values.

parameters.name: <ydata>
parameters.desc: Space-separated list of y-axis data values.

parameters.name: ?axis?
parameters.desc: Optional. Axis to use: "y" (default) or "y2".

cv_createWithFormula.description: Creates a new curve by applying a formula to the data of argument curves within the specified range. Setting a range bound to a non-numeric value (such as A) applies no limit in that direction.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Unique name for the new curve.

parameters.name: <formula>
parameters.desc: Formula or macro expression referencing argument curves with <curveName> syntax.

parameters.name: <xmin>
parameters.desc: Minimum x-value range, or a non-numeric value (e.g., "A") for no limit.

parameters.name: <xmax>
parameters.desc: Maximum x-value range, or a non-numeric value (e.g., "A") for no limit.

parameters.name: <ymin>
parameters.desc: Minimum y-value range, or a non-numeric value (e.g., "A") for no limit.

parameters.name: <ymax>
parameters.desc: Maximum y-value range, or a non-numeric value (e.g., "A") for no limit.

cv_createWithFormula.example: cv_createWithFormula f3 "<f1>+<f2>+10" A A A A

cv_delete.description: Deletes the specified curve from the current Inspect session.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to delete.

cv_display.description: Displays a previously created curve on the plot using the specified y-axis.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to display.

parameters.name: ?axis?
parameters.desc: Optional. Axis to use: "y" (default) or "y2".

cv_logScale.description: Creates a new curve where all values on the specified axis are transformed using the natural logarithm (ln).

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to transform.

parameters.name: <newCurveName>
parameters.desc: Name for the new curve with log-scaled values.

parameters.name: ?axis?
parameters.desc: Optional. Axis on which to apply the log scale: "x" (default) or "y".

cv_log10Scale.description: Creates a new curve where all values on the specified axis are transformed using the base-10 logarithm (log10).

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to transform.

parameters.name: <newCurveName>
parameters.desc: Name for the new curve with log10-scaled values.

parameters.name: ?axis?
parameters.desc: Optional. Axis on which to apply the log10 scale: "x" (default) or "y".

cv_split.description: Splits the input curve into multiple curves at points where the x-values are nonmonotonic (x[i+1] < x[i]). The number of names provided must match the actual number of resulting curves. The new curves are mapped onto the specified y-axis.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to split.

parameters.name: <axis>
parameters.desc: Y-axis to map the new curves onto (e.g., "y" or "y2").

parameters.name: <newCurveList>
parameters.desc: List of names for the new curves. The count must match the actual number of split curves.

cv_split_disc.description: Splits the input curve into multiple curves at points where discontinuities occur (x[i+1] == x[i] and y[i+1] != y[i]). The number of names provided must match the actual number of resulting curves.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to split.

parameters.name: <axis>
parameters.desc: Y-axis to map the new curves onto (e.g., "y" or "y2").

parameters.name: <newCurveList>
parameters.desc: List of names for the new curves. The count must match the actual number of split curves.

cv_getVals.description: Returns a nested list containing both the x-data and y-data of the specified curve. The x-data is the first element and y-data is the second element of the returned list.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve whose data is retrieved.

cv_getValsX.description: Returns a list of the x-data values for the specified curve.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve whose x-data is retrieved.

cv_getValsY.description: Returns a list of the y-data values for the specified curve.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve whose y-data is retrieved.

cv_getXaxis.description: Returns the project name and dataset ID of the x-dataset for the specified curve. The project name is the first element and the dataset ID is the second element of the returned list.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to query.

cv_getYaxis.description: Returns the project name and dataset ID of the y-dataset for the specified curve, in the same format as cv_getXaxis.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to query.

cv_printVals.description: Writes the x-data and y-data of a curve to standard output.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to print.

cv_abs.description: Replaces negative values of the x- or y-dataset by their absolute values. The axis argument specifies which dataset is affected.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to transform.

parameters.name: <axis>
parameters.desc: Axis to apply absolute value to: "x" or "y".

cv_delPts.description: Deletes the specified data points from the displayed set of curve points by their indices.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

parameters.name: <indexList>
parameters.desc: List of indices of the curve points to delete.

cv_inv.description: Reflects a curve about the specified axis, effectively negating the values on that axis.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to reflect.

parameters.name: <axis>
parameters.desc: Axis to reflect about: "x" or "y".

cv_reset.description: Restores the original appearance of a curve after a transformation has been applied.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to restore.

cv_lineColor.description: Sets the line color of the specified curve.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

parameters.name: <color>
parameters.desc: Line color name: "red", "blue", "green", "black", "white", "magenta", "cyan", "yellow", "orange", "brown", "violet", "gray", or "defcolor".

cv_lineStyle.description: Sets the line drawing style of the specified curve.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

parameters.name: <style>
parameters.desc: Line style: "solid", "dashed", "dotted", "long dashed", or "long dotted".

cv_renameCurve.description: Renames a curve to a new name.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Current name of the curve.

parameters.name: <newName>
parameters.desc: New name for the curve.

cv_set_interpol.description: Sets the interpolation method applied to each dataset of a curve on the specified axis.

parameters.name: <curveId>
parameters.desc: Name or identification of the curve.

parameters.name: <axis>
parameters.desc: Axis on which interpolation is set: "X" or "Y".

parameters.name: <type>
parameters.desc: Interpolation method: "AUTO" (default), "LIN" (linear), or "LOG" (logarithmic).

cv_setCurveAttr.description: Sets all curve-drawing attributes at once, including legend text, line color and style, marker shape and size, and marker outline and fill colors.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

parameters.name: <legend>
parameters.desc: Legend text displayed for the curve.

parameters.name: <color>
parameters.desc: Line color name (e.g., "red", "black", "blue").

parameters.name: <style>
parameters.desc: Line style: "solid", "dashed", "dotted", "long dashed", or "long dotted".

parameters.name: <width>
parameters.desc: Line width in pixels.

parameters.name: <shape>
parameters.desc: Marker shape: "none", "circle", "cross", "diamond", "plus", "scross", "splus", "square", or "triangle".

parameters.name: <size>
parameters.desc: Marker size in pixels.

parameters.name: <outColor>
parameters.desc: Marker outline color name.

parameters.name: <outWidth>
parameters.desc: Marker outline width in pixels.

parameters.name: <fillColor>
parameters.desc: Marker fill color name.

cv_setCurveAttr.example: cv_setCurveAttr idvd0 "Vg=0.0" black solid 1 circle 5 defcolor 1 defcolor

gb_setpreferences.description: Sets a preference option to a new value. Supported options are "precision" (integer, controls the number of decimal places shown in the status line) and "old_interpolation" (0 or 1, activates or deactivates the old interpolation method).

parameters.name: <type>
parameters.desc: Preference option name: "precision" or "old_interpolation".

parameters.name: <val>
parameters.desc: New value for the preference option.

gr_createLabel.description: Creates a text label at the specified coordinates in the plot area.

parameters.name: <label>
parameters.desc: Label text to display.

parameters.name: <coordx>
parameters.desc: X-coordinate of the label in plot coordinates.

parameters.name: <coordy>
parameters.desc: Y-coordinate of the label in plot coordinates.

parameters.name: <fontStr>
parameters.desc: Font specification string.

parameters.name: <color>
parameters.desc: Label text color name.

gr_deleteLabel.description: Deletes a previously created label from the plot area using its label ID.

parameters.name: <labelId>
parameters.desc: ID of the label to delete, as returned by gr_createLabel.

gr_formatAxis.description: Changes the number format of the displayed axis tick labels.

parameters.name: <axis>
parameters.desc: Axis to format: "X", "Y", or "Y2".

parameters.name: <format>
parameters.desc: Number format: "default", "engineering", "fixed", "scientific", "LOG", or "LIN".

gr_mappedAxis.description: Controls the visibility of the specified y-axis on the plot.

parameters.name: <axis>
parameters.desc: Y-axis to control: "y" or "y2".

parameters.name: <yesno>
parameters.desc: Axis visibility: "True" to show or "False" to hide.

gr_precision.description: Sets the numeric precision (number of decimal places) for the tick labels of the specified axis.

parameters.name: <axis>
parameters.desc: Axis to configure: "X", "Y", or "Y2".

parameters.name: <prec>
parameters.desc: Number of decimal places for axis tick labels.

gr_setAxisAttr.description: Sets all axis attributes including title, font, range, color, tick labels, and scale type.

parameters.name: <axis>
parameters.desc: Axis to configure: "X", "Y", or "Y2".

parameters.name: <title>
parameters.desc: Axis title text.

parameters.name: <tfont>
parameters.desc: Font size of the axis title.

parameters.name: <min>
parameters.desc: Minimum value of the axis range, or empty {} for auto.

parameters.name: <max>
parameters.desc: Maximum value of the axis range, or empty {} for auto.

parameters.name: <color>
parameters.desc: Color of the axis line.

parameters.name: <width>
parameters.desc: Width of the axis line.

parameters.name: <font>
parameters.desc: Font size of the tick labels.

parameters.name: <angle>
parameters.desc: Angle at which tick labels are drawn.

parameters.name: <div>
parameters.desc: Number of secondary ticks between major ticks.

parameters.name: <scale>
parameters.desc: Scale type: "lin" (linear) or "log" (logarithmic).

parameters.name: <tcolor>
parameters.desc: Color of the axis title text.

gr_setGeneralAttr.description: Sets general plot attributes such as frame visibility, axis tightness, background color, and selected curve color.

parameters.name: ?showFrame?
parameters.desc: Optional. Show plot frame; default is true.

parameters.name: ?axesTight?
parameters.desc: Optional. Tighten axes to data range; default is true.

parameters.name: ?backColor?
parameters.desc: Optional. Plot background color; default is "white".

parameters.name: ?selectColor?
parameters.desc: Optional. Color of the selected curve highlight; default is "cyan".

gr_setGridAttr.description: Sets grid attributes including visibility, alignment, minor ticks, style, color, and line width.

parameters.name: ?showGrid?
parameters.desc: Optional. Show grid lines; default is false.

parameters.name: ?gridAlign?
parameters.desc: Optional. Grid alignment: "left" (default) or "right".

parameters.name: ?minorTicks?
parameters.desc: Optional. Show minor tick marks; default is false.

parameters.name: ?gridStyle?
parameters.desc: Optional. Grid line style: "dashed", "dot-dashed", "dotted", "long dashed", "long dotted", "short dashed" (default), or "solid".

parameters.name: ?gridColor?
parameters.desc: Optional. Color of the grid lines; default is "black".

parameters.name: ?gridWidth?
parameters.desc: Optional. Thickness of the grid lines; default is 1.

gr_setLegendAttr.description: Sets all legend attributes including visibility, font, colors, frame position, and anchor point.

parameters.name: ?showFlag?
parameters.desc: Optional. Show legend; default is true.

parameters.name: ?fontName?
parameters.desc: Optional. Legend font name; default is "helvetica".

parameters.name: ?fontSize?
parameters.desc: Optional. Legend font size; default is 10.

parameters.name: ?fontStyle?
parameters.desc: Optional. Font style: "bold", "italic", "overstrike", "underline", or {} (default).

parameters.name: ?backColor?
parameters.desc: Optional. Legend background color; default is "white".

parameters.name: ?foreColor?
parameters.desc: Optional. Legend foreground (text) color; default is "black".

parameters.name: ?frameColor?
parameters.desc: Optional. Legend frame border color; default is "black".

parameters.name: ?frameWidth?
parameters.desc: Optional. Legend frame border width; default is 1.

parameters.name: ?framePos?
parameters.desc: Optional. Legend frame position: "left", "right" (default), "top", "bottom", "free", or "plot".

parameters.name: ?frameAnchor?
parameters.desc: Optional. Legend anchor point: "n" (default), "e", "s", "w", "ne", "se", "sw", or "nw".

gr_setLegendPos.description: Changes the position of the displayed legend by specifying new x and y coordinates in the plot area.

parameters.name: <x>
parameters.desc: New x-coordinate for the legend.

parameters.name: <y>
parameters.desc: New y-coordinate for the legend.

gr_setTitleAttr.description: Sets the plot title text and its font size and justification.

parameters.name: <title>
parameters.desc: Title text for the plot.

parameters.name: ?fontSize?
parameters.desc: Optional. Font size of the title; default is 14.

parameters.name: ?just?
parameters.desc: Optional. Title justification: "center" (default), "left", or "right".

cv_compute.description: Computes a scalar value by evaluating the given formula within the specified x and y range. Use "A" for a range bound to indicate no limit.

parameters.name: <formula>
parameters.desc: Formula string to evaluate, using <curveName> syntax to reference curve data.

parameters.name: <xmin>
parameters.desc: Minimum x-value range, or "A" for no limit.

parameters.name: <xmax>
parameters.desc: Maximum x-value range, or "A" for no limit.

parameters.name: <ymin>
parameters.desc: Minimum y-value range, or "A" for no limit.

parameters.name: <ymax>
parameters.desc: Maximum y-value range, or "A" for no limit.

cv_getZero.description: Computes the x-coordinate where the curve intersects the x-axis (y = 0) within the specified range. Returns an empty string if the curve does not cross the x-axis.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

parameters.name: <xmin>
parameters.desc: Minimum x-value range, or "A" for no limit.

parameters.name: <xmax>
parameters.desc: Maximum x-value range, or "A" for no limit.

parameters.name: <ymin>
parameters.desc: Minimum y-value range, or "A" for no limit.

parameters.name: <ymax>
parameters.desc: Maximum y-value range, or "A" for no limit.

cv_getZero.example: set voc [cv_compute "veczero(<j($n)>)" A A A A]

macro_define.description: Defines a reusable macro that can be used in formula expressions. Use <c n> as placeholders for curve arguments and <s n> for scalar arguments, where n starts at 1.

parameters.name: <macroName>
parameters.desc: Name of the macro to define.

parameters.name: <macroDef>
parameters.desc: Macro definition string using <c n> for curve placeholders and <s n> for scalar placeholders.

script_break.description: Suspends the execution of a script and passes control to the user interface. Script execution can be resumed by choosing Script > Continue Script.

script_exit.description: Stops the execution of a script and exits Inspect.

script_sleep.description: Pauses the execution of a script for a specified number of seconds.

parameters.name: <sec>
parameters.desc: Time to sleep in seconds.

f_Gamma.description: Computes the body-effect parameter Gamma at two different source-substrate voltages using the formula: Gamma = (VT2 - VT1) / (sqrt(const + VB2) - sqrt(const + VB1)).

parameters.name: <VT1>
parameters.desc: First threshold voltage value [V].

parameters.name: <VT2>
parameters.desc: Second threshold voltage value [V].

parameters.name: <VB1>
parameters.desc: First source-substrate voltage [V].

parameters.name: <VB2>
parameters.desc: Second source-substrate voltage [V].

parameters.name: ?const?
parameters.desc: 2*phiF where phiF is the Fermi-level potential. Default is 0.8 V.

f_gm.description: Computes the maximum transconductance [A/V] for a given Id-Vg curve.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the Id-Vg curve used to calculate gm.

parameters.name: ?xmin?
parameters.desc: Minimum x-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?xmax?
parameters.desc: Maximum x-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?ymin?
parameters.desc: Minimum y-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?ymax?
parameters.desc: Maximum y-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

f_hideInternalCurves.description: Hides the internally used curves created by the extraction function commands.

f_IDSS.description: Computes the saturation current from an Id-Vd curve at a fixed gate-source voltage.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the Id-Vd curve at the fixed gate-source voltage.

parameters.name: ?xmin?
parameters.desc: Minimum x-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?xmax?
parameters.desc: Maximum x-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?ymin?
parameters.desc: Minimum y-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?ymax?
parameters.desc: Maximum y-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

f_KP.description: Computes the transconductance parameter KP [A/V^2] from a given transconductance value and drain-source voltage.

parameters.name: <gm>
parameters.desc: Transconductance value [A/V].

parameters.name: ?VDS?
parameters.desc: Drain-source voltage [V]. Default is 0.1.

f_Ron.description: Computes the on-state resistance [kOhm] in the linear region from an Id-Vd curve.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the Id-Vd curve at the fixed gate-source voltage.

parameters.name: ?xmin?
parameters.desc: Minimum x-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?xmax?
parameters.desc: Maximum x-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?ymin?
parameters.desc: Minimum y-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?ymax?
parameters.desc: Maximum y-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

f_Rout.description: Computes the output resistance [kOhm] in the saturation region from an Id-Vd curve.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the Id-Vd curve at the fixed gate-source voltage.

parameters.name: ?xmin?
parameters.desc: Minimum x-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?xmax?
parameters.desc: Maximum x-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?ymin?
parameters.desc: Minimum y-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?ymax?
parameters.desc: Maximum y-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

f_showInternalCurves.description: Displays the internally used curves created by the extraction function commands on the specified axis.

parameters.name: ?axis?
parameters.desc: Axis to display the internal curves on. Default is "left".

f_TetaG.description: Computes the mobility modulation parameter TetaG [V^-1] using the formula: TetaG = gm(VGSlow)/ID(VGShigh) - 1/(VGShigh - VT).

parameters.name: <VT>
parameters.desc: Threshold voltage value [V].

parameters.name: <gm>
parameters.desc: Transconductance value [A/V].

parameters.name: <idvgs>
parameters.desc: Name of the Id-Vg curve.

parameters.name: <vgsvgs>
parameters.desc: Name of the Vg-Vg curve.

parameters.name: ?xmin?
parameters.desc: Minimum x-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?xmax?
parameters.desc: Maximum x-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?ymin?
parameters.desc: Minimum y-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?ymax?
parameters.desc: Maximum y-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

f_VT.description: Computes the threshold voltage [V] of a given curve using the maximum slope intercept method: VT = intercept(maxslope(curve)).

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

parameters.name: ?xmin?
parameters.desc: Minimum x-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?xmax?
parameters.desc: Maximum x-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?ymin?
parameters.desc: Minimum y-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?ymax?
parameters.desc: Maximum y-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

f_VT1.description: Computes the threshold voltage [V] of a given curve, typically extracted at Id = 0.1 uA/um.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

parameters.name: ?xmin?
parameters.desc: Minimum x-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?xmax?
parameters.desc: Maximum x-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?ymin?
parameters.desc: Minimum y-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?ymax?
parameters.desc: Maximum y-value for the computation range. Default is the full curve range.

f_VT2.description: Computes the threshold voltage [V] of a given curve using the intersection of maxslope and minslope lines in the logarithmic curve.

parameters.name: <curveName>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

cv_linTransCurve.description: Applies a linear transformation to the X- and Y-values of a curve, replacing them with X = X*Xm + Xb and Y = Y*Ym + Yb.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to transform.

parameters.name: <Xm>
parameters.desc: X-axis multiplication factor.

parameters.name: <Xb>
parameters.desc: X-axis offset (additive shift).

parameters.name: <Ym>
parameters.desc: Y-axis multiplication factor.

parameters.name: <Yb>
parameters.desc: Y-axis offset (additive shift).

parameters.name: <axis>
parameters.desc: Y-axis to display the transformed curve on: "y" or "y2".

cv_scaleCurve.description: Scales the X- and Y-values of a curve by the given factors, multiplying X-values by XFactor and Y-values by YFactor.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to scale.

parameters.name: <XFactor>
parameters.desc: X-axis scaling factor.

parameters.name: <YFactor>
parameters.desc: Y-axis scaling factor.

parameters.name: <axis>
parameters.desc: Y-axis to display the scaled curve on: "y" or "y2".

ExtractBVi.description: Extracts the breakdown voltage [V] at which the current reaches a specified level from a breakdown curve.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name of the extracted parameter.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Name of the breakdown curve.

parameters.name: <Ilevel>
parameters.desc: Current level [A] at which the breakdown voltage is extracted.

ExtractBVv.description: Extracts the breakdown voltage [V] defined as the maximum voltage that can be applied to a contact.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name of the extracted parameter.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Name of the breakdown curve.

parameters.name: <sign>
parameters.desc: Transistor type sign: +1 for n-p-n, -1 for p-n-p (use -1 if breakdown occurs at negative bias).

ExtractEarlyV.description: Extracts the Early voltage [V] from an Ic-Vce curve by computing the slope at a specified bias point.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name of the extracted parameter.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Name of the Ic-Vce curve.

parameters.name: <Vtarget>
parameters.desc: Bias point [V] at which the slope is determined for the Early voltage computation.

ExtractGm.description: Extracts the maximum transconductance [A/V] from an Id-Vgs curve.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name of the extracted parameter.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Name of the Id-Vgs curve.

parameters.name: ?type?
parameters.desc: Transistor type: "nMOS", "nMOSneg", "pMOS", or "pMOSneg". If omitted, the type is determined automatically.

ExtractGmb.description: Extracts the maximum transconductance from an Id-Vgs curve using parabolic interpolation for better accuracy with limited sample points.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name of the extracted parameter.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Name of the Id-Vgs curve.

parameters.name: ?type?
parameters.desc: Transistor type: "nMOS", "nMOSneg", "pMOS", or "pMOSneg". If omitted, the type is determined automatically.

ExtractIoff.description: Extracts the drain leakage current [A] from an Id-Vgs curve at a specified gate voltage.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name of the extracted parameter.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Name of the Id-Vgs curve (computed for a high drain bias).

parameters.name: <Voff>
parameters.desc: Gate voltage [V] at which the drain leakage current is extracted, typically a small nonzero value to avoid noise.

ExtractMax.description: Extracts the maximum y-value of a curve.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name of the extracted parameter.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

ExtractRon.description: Extracts the on-state resistance [Ohm*um] from an Id-Vds curve at a specified drain voltage.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name of the extracted parameter.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Name of the Id-Vds curve (computed for a high gate bias).

parameters.name: <Von>
parameters.desc: Drain voltage [V] at which the on-state resistance is extracted, typically well beyond saturation.

ExtractSS.description: Extracts the subthreshold voltage swing [mV/dec] from an Id-Vgs curve at a specified gate voltage.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name of the extracted parameter.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Name of the Id-Vgs curve.

parameters.name: <Vgo>
parameters.desc: Gate voltage [V] at which the slope is extracted. Should be well below the threshold voltage.

ExtractValue.description: Extracts the y-value of a curve at a given x-point.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name of the extracted parameter.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

parameters.name: <Xo>
parameters.desc: X-point at which the y-value is extracted.

ExtractVtgm.description: Extracts the threshold voltage [V] from an Id-Vgs curve using the maximum transconductance method.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name of the extracted parameter as it appears in Sentaurus Workbench.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Name of the Id-Vgs curve.

parameters.name: ?type?
parameters.desc: Transistor type: "nMOS", "nMOSneg", "pMOS", or "pMOSneg". If omitted, the type is determined automatically by analyzing the curve.

ExtractVtgmb.description: Extracts the threshold voltage from an Id-Vgs curve using the maximum transconductance method with parabolic interpolation for better accuracy with limited gate-bias sample points.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name of the extracted parameter as it appears in Sentaurus Workbench.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Name of the Id-Vgs curve.

parameters.name: ?type?
parameters.desc: Transistor type: "nMOS", "nMOSneg", "pMOS", or "pMOSneg". If omitted, the type is determined automatically.

ExtractVti.description: Extracts the gate voltage [V] from an Id-Vgs curve at which the drain current exceeds a given current level.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name of the extracted parameter.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Name of the Id-Vgs curve.

parameters.name: <Ilevel>
parameters.desc: Drain current level [A] at which the gate voltage is extracted.

FilterTable.description: Processes Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree data to create a plot of one parameter as a function of another for a subset of experiments matching specified conditions.

parameters.name: <XList>
parameters.desc: Tcl list of Sentaurus Workbench parameter values used as x-values.

parameters.name: <YList>
parameters.desc: Tcl list of Sentaurus Workbench parameter values used as y-values.

parameters.name: ?conditionTarget1?
parameters.desc: Target value that experiments must match to be included in the plot.

parameters.name: ?conditionList1?
parameters.desc: Tcl list of Sentaurus Workbench parameter values used as filter condition.

parameters.name: ?conditionTarget2?
parameters.desc: Second condition target value.

parameters.name: ?conditionList2?
parameters.desc: Second condition Tcl list.

parameters.name: ?conditionTarget3?
parameters.desc: Third condition target value.

parameters.name: ?conditionList3?
parameters.desc: Third condition Tcl list.

cv_addCurve.description: Adds the y-values of curve cname2 to the y-values of curve cname.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the target curve.

parameters.name: <cname2>
parameters.desc: Name of the source curve whose y-values are added.

cv_addDataset.description: Adds the y-values of a dataset to an existing curve.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to which the dataset will be added.

parameters.name: <xdset>
parameters.desc: Dataset name for the X-values to be added.

parameters.name: <ydset>
parameters.desc: Dataset name for the Y-values to be added.

cv_addDataset.example: cv_addDataset iv "n4_des pContact OuterVoltage" "n4_des nContact TotalCurrent"

cv_angularMap.description: Maps a periodic curve to a fixed angular range, wrapping data points that exceed the range back into the first period.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

parameters.name: ?astart?
parameters.desc: Start of the angular range. Default is 0.

parameters.name: ?aend?
parameters.desc: End of the angular range. Default is 360.

cv_linTrans.description: Performs a linear transformation on a curve, replacing x with xm*x+xb and y with ym*y+yb.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

parameters.name: <xm>
parameters.desc: Slope for the x-values.

parameters.name: ?xb?
parameters.desc: Offset for the x-values. Default is 0.

parameters.name: ?ym?
parameters.desc: Slope for the y-values. Default is 1.

parameters.name: ?yb?
parameters.desc: Offset for the y-values. Default is 0.

cv_linTrans.example:
# Scale IV curve from A/V to mA/mV, remove 2A offset
cv_linTrans iv 1e3 0 1e3 -2000

cv_monotonicX.description: Extracts the portion of a curve where x-values increase monotonically to the maximal x-value, removing decreasing segments.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

cv_scale.description: Scales a curve linearly by multiplying x-values by xm and y-values by ym.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

parameters.name: <xm>
parameters.desc: Scale factor applied to x-values.

parameters.name: <ym>
parameters.desc: Scale factor applied to y-values.

cv_scale.example:
# Scale current over time from A/s to mA/us
cv_scale i_t 1e6 1e3

cv_sort.description: Sorts data points of a curve by x-values in ascending order and removes duplicate x-values.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

parameters.name: ?xdigits?
parameters.desc: Number of digits used to determine if two x-values are identical. Default is 20.

cv_autoIncrStyle.description: Sets the curve attributes to be automatically incremented each time a curve is displayed with cv_disp. The attributes are incremented in the order given by stylelist.

parameters.name: ?stylelist?
parameters.desc: List of style attributes to increment: {color fillColor line symbol}. Default is {color fillColor line symbol}.

parameters.name: ?off?
parameters.desc: Pass "off" to switch off automatic increment.

cv_autoIncrStyle.example:
# Increment color first, then symbol
cv_autoIncrStyle {color symbol}
cv_disp c1
cv_disp c2

cv_disp.description: Displays a curve using the specified label and axis. Curve attributes are incremented by default so that each displayed curve can be easily distinguished.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

parameters.name: ?label?
parameters.desc: Label to display in the legend. Default is the curve name.

parameters.name: ?axis?
parameters.desc: Axis to use: "y" (default) or "y2".

cv_nextColor.description: Advances to the next color from the extend::COLORPALETTE list, or selects a specific color by index.

parameters.name: ?cindex?
parameters.desc: If specified, selects the entry at this index from the color palette. Otherwise, the next entry is chosen.

cv_nextColor.example:
cv_autoIncrStyle off
cv_disp iv1
cv_nextColor
cv_disp iv2

cv_nextLine.description: Advances to the next line style from the extend::LINEPALETTE list, or selects a specific style by index.

parameters.name: ?cindex?
parameters.desc: If specified, selects the entry at this index from the line palette. Otherwise, the next entry is chosen.

cv_nextLine.example:
cv_autoIncrStyle off
cv_disp iv1
cv_nextLine
cv_disp iv2

cv_nextSymbol.description: Advances to the next symbol type from the extend::SYMBOLPALETTE list, or selects a specific symbol by index.

parameters.name: ?cindex?
parameters.desc: If specified, selects the entry at this index from the symbol palette. Otherwise, the next entry is chosen.

cv_nextSymbol.example:
cv_autoIncrStyle off
cv_disp iv1
cv_nextSymbol
cv_disp iv2

cv_resetColor.description: Resets the curve color to the default entry (the first entry from the extend::COLORPALETTE list).
cv_resetColor.example:
cv_autoIncrStyle off
cv_disp iv1
cv_nextColor
cv_disp iv2
cv_resetColor
cv_disp iv3

cv_resetFillColor.description: Resets the fill color of curve symbols to white.
cv_resetFillColor.example:
cv_autoIncrStyle off
cv_disp iv1
cv_nextColor
cv_disp iv2
cv_resetFillColor
cv_disp iv3

cv_resetLine.description: Resets the line style to the default entry (the first entry from the extend::LINEPALETTE list).
cv_resetLine.example:
cv_autoIncrStyle off
cv_disp iv1
cv_nextLine
cv_disp iv2
cv_resetLine
cv_disp iv3

cv_resetStyle.description: Resets all curve style attributes (color, fill color, symbol, and line style) to their default values.
cv_resetStyle.example:
cv_autoIncrStyle off
cv_disp iv1
cv_nextSymbol
cv_nextColor
cv_disp iv2
cv_resetStyle
cv_disp iv3

cv_resetSymbol.description: Resets the symbol to the default entry (the first entry from the extend::SYMBOLPALETTE list).
cv_resetSymbol.example:
cv_autoIncrStyle off
cv_disp iv1
cv_nextSymbol
cv_disp iv2
cv_resetSymbol
cv_disp iv3

cv_setFillColor.description: Switches the fill color of curve symbols on or off.

parameters.name: ?mode?
parameters.desc: 1 fills the symbol with the curve color (default), 0 sets the fill color to white.

cv_setFillColor.example:
cv_autoIncrStyle off
cv_setSymbol 1
cv_disp iv1
cv_nextColor
cv_setFillColor 1
cv_disp iv2

cv_setSymbol.description: Switches the display of symbols on curve data points on or off.

parameters.name: ?mode?
parameters.desc: 1 shows symbols (default), 0 hides symbols.

cv_setSymbol.example:
cv_autoIncrStyle off
cv_disp iv1
cv_nextColor
cv_setSymbol 1
cv_disp iv2

cv_exists.description: Checks whether a curve with the given name exists.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to check.

cv_getGlobalExtrema.description: Returns the global maximum or minimum of a curve as a list of {x y} values.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

parameters.name: ?type?
parameters.desc: "max" for global maximum (default) or "min" for global minimum.

cv_getGlobalExtrema.example:
set cmin [cv_getGlobalExtrema iv min]
puts "The minimum of the iv-curve is [lindex $cmin 1] at [lindex $cmin 0]"

cv_getLocalExtrema.description: Returns all local maxima or minima of a curve as a list of {x y} pairs.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

parameters.name: ?type?
parameters.desc: "max" for local maxima (default) or "min" for local minima.

cv_getLocalExtrema.example:
set cmax [cv_getLocalExtrema iv "max"]
puts "All maxima of the iv-curve: $cmax"

cv_getNames.description: Returns a list of all existing curve names.

cv_getRange.description: Returns the x- and y-range of a curve as a list {xmin xmax ymin ymax}.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

cv_getXmax.description: Returns the upper boundary (maximum) of the x-values of a curve.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

cv_getXmax.example: puts "upper boundary of the x values of iv: [cv_getXmax iv]"

cv_getXmin.description: Returns the lower boundary (minimum) of the x-values of a curve.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

cv_getXmin.example: puts "lower boundary of the x values of iv: [cv_getXmin iv]"

cv_getYmax.description: Returns the upper boundary (maximum) of the y-values of a curve.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

cv_getYmax.example: puts "upper boundary of the y values of iv: [cv_getYmax iv]"

cv_getYmin.description: Returns the lower boundary (minimum) of the y-values of a curve.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

cv_getYmin.example: puts "lower boundary of the y values of iv: [cv_getYmin iv]"

cv_isVisible.description: Checks whether a curve is currently displayed.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

cv_integrate.description: Performs numerical integration of a formula and returns the integration value.

parameters.name: <formula>
parameters.desc: Formula to be integrated, as specified for cv_createWithFormula.

parameters.name: ?xstart?
parameters.desc: Start of the integration interval. Default uses the curve start.

parameters.name: ?xend?
parameters.desc: End of the integration interval. Default uses the curve end.

parameters.name: ?mode?
parameters.desc: Integration mode: {} (Inspect internal, default), "sumup" (sum all y-values), or "trapez" (trapezoidal rule).

parameters.name: ?xdigits?
parameters.desc: Number of digits to determine if two x-values are identical.

cv_integrate.example:
# P_t is the time-dependent power
puts "energy in the first second is: [cv_integrate P_t 0 1 trapez]"

cv_linFit.description: Performs a linear fit y = A + B*x to a curve formula and returns fit statistics including intercept, slope, R-squared, and standard errors.

parameters.name: <formula>
parameters.desc: Formula to be fitted, as specified for cv_createWithFormula.

parameters.name: ?xstart?
parameters.desc: Start of the fit interval. Default uses the full curve range.

parameters.name: ?xend?
parameters.desc: End of the fit interval. Default uses the full curve range.

cv_round.description: Rounds off the x-data and y-data values of a curve to the specified number of digits.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve.

parameters.name: <xdigits>
parameters.desc: Number of digits to keep for x-values. -1 means no rounding.

parameters.name: <ydigits>
parameters.desc: Number of digits to keep for y-values. -1 means no rounding.

fi_readTxtFile.description: Reads data from an ASCII text file where columns are separated by spaces, creating a curve with x-values from the first column and y-values from the specified column.

parameters.name: <fname>
parameters.desc: Name of the ASCII file to read.

parameters.name: <cname>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve to be created.

parameters.name: ?columnIdx?
parameters.desc: Column index for y-values (0-based). Default is 1 (second column).

fi_readTxtFileHeader.description: Reads and returns the header line from an ASCII file (a single line that is neither data nor comment).

parameters.name: <fname>
parameters.desc: Name of the ASCII file.

proj_check.description: Checks all datasets in a project to verify whether all entries contain valid numeric data.

parameters.name: <proj>
parameters.desc: Project name of the loaded .plt file.

proj_datasetExists.description: Checks whether a project contains data with the specified dataset name and optional group name.

parameters.name: <proj>
parameters.desc: Project name of the loaded .plt file.

parameters.name: <datasetName>
parameters.desc: Name of the dataset.

parameters.name: ?groupId?
parameters.desc: Group name of the dataset. If omitted and datasetName contains a space, the first word is used as the group name.

proj_datasetExists.example:
proj_load n5_des.plt
if {[proj_datasetExists n5_des "anode OuterVoltage"]} { puts "anode OuterVoltage exists" }

proj_getGroups.description: Returns a sorted list of all group names in a project.

parameters.name: <proj>
parameters.desc: Project name of the loaded .plt file.

proj_getGroups.example:
proj_load n5_des.plt
puts "all group names: [proj_getGroups n5_des]"

proj_groupExists.description: Checks whether a project contains a particular group.

parameters.name: <proj>
parameters.desc: Project name of the loaded .plt file.

parameters.name: <groupName>
parameters.desc: Name of the group to check.

proj_groupExists.example:
proj_load n5_des.plt
if {[proj_groupExists n5_des "anode"]} { puts "group anode exists" }

proj_loadPlx.description: Loads data from a .plx file and creates a curve without displaying it.

parameters.name: <fileName>
parameters.desc: Name of the .plx file to load.

parameters.name: ?curveName?
parameters.desc: Name for the created curve. Default uses the dataset name.

parameters.name: ?appendDatasetName?
parameters.desc: If 1, appends the dataset name to the curve name in parentheses.

gr_axis.description: Sets the attributes of the x-axis, y-axis, or y2-axis including label, range, and scaling.

parameters.name: <axis>
parameters.desc: Axis to modify: "x", "y", or "y2".

parameters.name: <title>
parameters.desc: Axis label.

parameters.name: ?xmin?
parameters.desc: Lower range bound. {} specifies automatic scaling.

parameters.name: ?xmax?
parameters.desc: Upper range bound. {} specifies automatic scaling.

parameters.name: ?scale?
parameters.desc: Scaling type: "lin" (default) or "log".

gr_resetAxis.description: Resets all axis attributes to their defaults and switches off the y2-axis.

gr_setStyle.description: Sets the style of the plot area, controlling font sizes and layout for different use cases.

parameters.name: <mode>
parameters.desc: Plot style: "screen" (default, for interactive use) or "presentation" (larger fonts for presentations).

ldiff.description: Returns all items of list1 that are not in list2. With the symmetric flag, also includes items of list2 not in list1.

parameters.name: <list1>
parameters.desc: First list.

parameters.name: <list2>
parameters.desc: Second list.

parameters.name: ?symmetric?
parameters.desc: If specified, also returns items from list2 not in list1.

lintersect.description: Returns all items that are members of both list1 and list2.

parameters.name: <list1>
parameters.desc: First list.

parameters.name: <list2>
parameters.desc: Second list.

ltranspose.description: Transposes a list of sublists, converting rows to columns.

parameters.name: <list>
parameters.desc: List to transpose.

lunion.description: Returns a list of unique items that are members of list1 or list2.

parameters.name: <list1>
parameters.desc: First list.

parameters.name: <list2>
parameters.desc: Second list.

dbputs.description: Outputs a debug string to the log only when the global ::DEBUG variable is greater than or equal to the specified debug level.

parameters.name: <str>
parameters.desc: String to be printed to standard output.

parameters.name: ?dbglevel?
parameters.desc: Debug level threshold. Default is 1.

dbputs.example:
dbputs "test1"
set ::DEBUG 2
dbputs "test2"
dbputs "test3" 2
dbputs "test4" 3
set ::DEBUG 0
dbputs "test5"

ds_getValue.description: Returns the value at a specific index of a dataset within a loaded project.

parameters.name: <proj>
parameters.desc: Project name of the loaded .plt file.

parameters.name: <datasetName>
parameters.desc: Name of the dataset.

parameters.name: ?index?
parameters.desc: Index of the dataset item to return (0-based). Default is "end" (last value).

ds_getValue.example:
proj_load n5_des.plt
puts "first value [ds_getValue n5_des "anode OuterVoltage" 0]"
puts "last value [ds_getValue n5_des "anode OuterVoltage"]"

vecmax.description: Returns the maximum y-value of the given curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

vecmin.description: Returns the minimum y-value of the given curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

vecvalx.description: Returns the x-value at a given y-value of the specified curve. For curves with exponential behavior, better results are obtained by transforming the curve to logarithmic scale before applying this operator.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

parameters.name: <yValue>
parameters.desc: Y-value for which to find the corresponding x-value.

vecvaly.description: Returns the y-value at a given x-value of the specified curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

parameters.name: <xValue>
parameters.desc: X-value for which to find the corresponding y-value.

veczero.description: Returns the x-value at which the curve crosses y = 0 (the x-intercept).

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

fftre.description: Returns the real part of the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) of the given curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve (real-valued).

fftim.description: Returns the imaginary part of the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) of the given curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve (real-valued).

fftabs.description: Returns a vector holding the absolute value (magnitude) of the given complex curve, computed from its real and imaginary parts.

parameters.name: <curve_real>
parameters.desc: Real part of the input curve.

parameters.name: <curve_imaginary>
parameters.desc: Imaginary part of the input curve.

ifftre.description: Returns the real part of the inverse Fast Fourier Transform (IFFT) of the given curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve (real-valued).

ifftim.description: Returns the imaginary part of the inverse Fast Fourier Transform (IFFT) of the given curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve (real-valued).

cfftre.description: Returns the real part of the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) of the given complex curve (specified by its real and imaginary parts).

parameters.name: <curve_real>
parameters.desc: Real part of the input curve.

parameters.name: <curve_imaginary>
parameters.desc: Imaginary part of the input curve.

cfftim.description: Returns the imaginary part of the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) of the given complex curve (specified by its real and imaginary parts).

parameters.name: <curve_real>
parameters.desc: Real part of the input curve.

parameters.name: <curve_imaginary>
parameters.desc: Imaginary part of the input curve.

cifftre.description: Returns the real part of the inverse FFT of the given complex curve (specified by its real and imaginary parts).

parameters.name: <curve_real>
parameters.desc: Real part of the input curve.

parameters.name: <curve_imaginary>
parameters.desc: Imaginary part of the input curve.

cifftim.description: Returns the imaginary part of the inverse FFT of the given complex curve (specified by its real and imaginary parts).

parameters.name: <curve_real>
parameters.desc: Real part of the input curve.

parameters.name: <curve_imaginary>
parameters.desc: Imaginary part of the input curve.

j0.description: Returns the Bessel function of the first kind of order 0 for each element of the curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

j1.description: Returns the Bessel function of the first kind of order 1 for each element of the curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

y0.description: Returns the Bessel function of the second kind of order 0 for each element of the curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

y1.description: Returns the Bessel function of the second kind of order 1 for each element of the curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

acosh.description: Returns the inverse hyperbolic cosine. Curve values must be greater than or equal to 1.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve (values >= 1).

asinh.description: Returns the inverse hyperbolic sine for each element of the curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

atanh.description: Returns the inverse hyperbolic tangent. Curve values must be between -1 and 1 (excluding -1 and 1).

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve (values in (-1, 1)).

cbrt.description: Returns the cube root for each element of the curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

diff.description: Returns the first derivative of the given curve. Requires a curve-only argument (a curve with more than one point).

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve (must have more than one point).

erf.description: Returns the error function for each element of the curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

fabs.description: Returns the absolute value for each element of the curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

gamma.description: Returns the Gamma function for each element of the curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

integr.description: Returns the integral of the given curve. Requires a curve-only argument (a curve with more than one point).

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve (must have more than one point).

intercept.description: Returns the x-intercept of the given curve, that is, the x-value where the curve crosses the x-axis. Commonly used with maxslope() to extract threshold voltage: VT = intercept(maxslope(curve)).

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

intercept.example:
set vt [f_VT idvgs]
# Equivalent formula: VT = intercept(maxslope(curve))

lgamma.description: Returns the natural logarithm of the absolute value of the Gamma function for each element of the curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

maxslope.description: Returns a scalar value representing the maximum slope of the given curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

minslope.description: Returns a scalar value representing the minimum slope of the given curve.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

tangent.description: Returns a curve that is tangent to the given curve at the specified x-value.

parameters.name: <curve>
parameters.desc: Input curve.

parameters.name: <xValue>
parameters.desc: X-value at which to compute the tangent line.

const::ElectronVolt.description: Electron volt energy unit. Value: 1.6021773349e-19 J.

const::FreeSpaceImpedance.description: Impedance of free space. Value: 376.730313462 Ohm.

const::MolarVolume.description: Molar volume of an ideal gas at STP. Value: 22.4141019e-3 m3/mol.

const::Permeability.description: Permeability of free space (magnetic constant). Value: 12.566370614e-7 H/m.

const::Permittivity.description: Permittivity of free space (electric constant). Value: 8.854187817e-12 F/m.

const::SpeedOfLight.description: Speed of light in vacuum. Value: 299792458 m/s.

const::getVarNames.description: Returns a list of all variable names defined in the PhysicalConstants library.

const::printVarNames.description: Prints the names of all available variables in the PhysicalConstants library to the standard output.

iccap_Write.description: Exports data to a file using the IC-CAP data management file format. The headerInfo is a list formed by the sublists userInput, iccapInput, and output. The data is an array of curve data indexed by input variable values.

parameters.name: <fileName>
parameters.desc: Output file name.

parameters.name: <headerInfo>
parameters.desc: Header information list containing userInput, iccapInput, and output sublists.

parameters.name: <data>
parameters.desc: Array of curve data (see Array Data in the documentation).

cvcmp_CompareTwoCurves.description: Computes the square difference between two curves within a given domain (window) using either linear scale or logarithmic scale.

parameters.name: <curve1>
parameters.desc: First curve name.

parameters.name: <curve2>
parameters.desc: Second curve name.

parameters.name: <windowX>
parameters.desc: Window size in the x-axis for comparison.

parameters.name: <use_log>
parameters.desc: True to use logarithmic scale, false for linear.

parameters.name: <n>
parameters.desc: Base name for the internal curves.

cvcmp_CompareTwoCurves.example:
load_library curvecomp
cvcmp_CompareTwoCurves curve1 curve2 1.0 0 comp

cvcmp_DeltaTwoCurves.description: Writes the square difference between two curves within a given domain (window) to the standard output. Both curves are read from files. Uses the ft_scale command to export the computed difference to the Family Tree of Sentaurus Workbench.

parameters.name: <exp_file>
parameters.desc: File containing the experimental curve.

parameters.name: <sim_file>
parameters.desc: File containing the simulation curve.

parameters.name: <minX>
parameters.desc: Minimum x-value of the comparison window.

parameters.name: <maxX>
parameters.desc: Maximum x-value of the comparison window.

parameters.name: <use_log>
parameters.desc: True to use logarithmic scale, false for linear.

parameters.name: <name>
parameters.desc: Name of the column in the Family Tree of Sentaurus Workbench where the result is stored.

cvcmp_DeltaTwoCurves.example:
load_library curvecomp
cvcmp_DeltaTwoCurves exp.dat sim.dat 0.0 5.0 0 delta

load_library.description: Loads a script library and makes all its functionality available. All commands of a particular library share a common prefix. Available libraries include: PhysicalConstants, ise2iccap, curvecomp, EXTRACT, extend, and user-defined site libraries.

parameters.name: <libraryName>
parameters.desc: Name of the library to load.
